name: Union Bank & Trust Company
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Southeast Arkansas offering checking, savings, mortgages, and online banking since 1887.
description_long: Union Bank & Trust Company was originally established as Monticello Bank in 1887 in Monticello, Arkansas. The institution merged with Citizens Bank of Monticello in 1915 to form the current entity, a consolidation born from their collaborative response to the 1907 financial panic. Over 135 years of continuous operation demonstrates stability and longevity in the Southeast Arkansas market, particularly in Drew County and surrounding areas. The bank is fully FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. Union Bank & Trust offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking products. Services include multiple checking account options tailored to different customer needs, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, online and mobile banking available 24/7, mortgage lending (conventional, FHA, VA, and USDA programs), business online banking enrollment, and financial calculators for customer education. The bank emphasizes accessibility through physical branch locations and digital platforms. The bank distinguishes itself as a locally-owned institution with ownership and staff comprising community members invested in Southeast Arkansas. They position themselves as early adopters of financial innovations in their region and prioritize personal service alongside industry-leading products. The bank actively engages in community efforts and emphasizes their commitment to local economic and educational development. As a small regional community bank, Union Bank & Trust serves a geographically limited market centered on Southeast Arkansas. While their century-plus history and local ownership provide relationship-banking advantages, customers outside their service area cannot access their products. The website provides limited detail on specific account features, interest rates, fees, or product terms, requiring direct contact for comprehensive information.
services: Specialized checking account options for different customer needs
pros: FDIC-insured with full U.S. Government backing for deposit protection
136+ years of continuous operation demonstrates institutional stability and longevity
Locally-owned with management and staff invested in the Southeast Arkansas community
24/7 online and mobile banking access for account management convenience
Multiple mortgage options including conventional, FHA, VA, and USDA programs
Specialized checking accounts designed for different customer needs
Early adopter of new financial innovations and products in their region
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Southeast Arkansas, primarily Drew County
Website lacks specific information on account fees, interest rates, and detailed product terms
No information provided about minimum balance requirements or account opening procedures
Limited details on competitive positioning or fee structures compared to other regional banks
Small institution may have fewer branch locations and ATM access than national banks
best_for: Southeast Arkansas residents seeking relationship-based community banking with local decision-making
Homebuyers in the service area needing mortgage loans including FHA, VA, or USDA programs
Business owners in Drew County requiring business checking and online banking solutions
Customers prioritizing FDIC-insured deposits and community-focused financial institutions
diagnosis: Union Bank & Trust is best for Southeast Arkansas residents and businesses seeking FDIC-insured community banking with local ownership and personalized service. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only serves a specific regional market, making it inaccessible to customers outside Southeast Arkansas, and limited online product disclosure requires direct contact for detailed account terms and rates.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
